title: chrysanthellum procumbens
intro: Chrysanthellum procumbens, also known as 'false chamomile', is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Chrysanthellum procumbens include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality. Chrysanthellum procumbens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments.
